text: Code reviews are an essential practice in software development. When a developer finishes a piece of code, another team member checks it thoroughly. This helps catch bugs early, before they reach the final product. Fixing a problem later in the process is much more expensive and time-consuming. Beyond finding mistakes, code reviews also promote knowledge sharing. Team members learn from each other's techniques and approaches, which helps everyone improve. Over time, this shared learning raises the overall quality of the team. A culture of respectful, constructive feedback strengthens collaboration and leads to better, more reliable software.
